assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS in consolidated financial statements.
The recognised intragroup dividend receivable creates foreign currency risk exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as a possible hedged item, which is relevant because the fact pattern refers to a recognised receivable in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a forecast transaction as a possible hedged item and specifically addresses when foreign currency risk on intragroup forecast transactions may qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRIC 16 on hedges of foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations, which is a distinct hedge accounting model relevant to intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.
